content:
# iter-007 → next-plan recommendations

## Closest-to-completion / prioritize
1. **`sheafifyTensorComparison` then `tensorAssoc`** (P1b-i″ tail). This is the ONE
   remaining brick of the monoidal scaffolding. Spec (from prover hand-off):
   `sheafifyTensorComparison {C} [Category C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat} (P Q :
   PresheafOfModules (R.comp (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) : Monoidal.tensorObj
   (sheafify P).val (sheafify Q).val ≅ (sheafify (Monoidal.tensorObj P Q)).val`
   (or the cleaner `SheafOfModules`-level `(sheafify P) ⊗ Q ≅ sheafify (P ⊗ Q)`).
   With it + presheaf `MonoidalCategory.associator` (defeq-available) + `sheafifyValIso`,
   `tensorAssoc` assembles like `tensorLeftUnitor`. **Substantial** (sheafification
   preserves the tensor) — likely its own iter; may itself need sub-decomposition.
2. **Once the full braiding+assoc+unitor scaffolding is real, dispatch a `prove` lane
   on `H_tensor` (1.1.3) and `H_tensor_invertible` (1.1.2).** These are the un-gated
   paper lemmas the whole P1b-i″ track exists to close (6→4). Their proofs are
   tensor-algebra + Yoneda over the now-concrete `homTensorFunctor`. Do NOT assign
   before `tensorAssoc` lands — the twist/right-exactness manipulation needs it.

## Coverage debt — BLUEPRINT THESE (planner authors prose; reviewer does not)
`archon dag-query unmatched` = 4 new `lean_aux` nodes (all proved, no sorry):
- **`presheafTensorBraiding`** (Basic.lean ~145). Presheaf-level braiding of
  `Monoidal.tensorObj` via `isoMk` + pointwise `β_`. Depends on:
  `PresheafOfModules.isoMk`, `…Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul`, `ModuleCat` symmetric
  braiding. → fold under / cite from `lem:tensorMod_braiding`.
- **`presheafLeftUnitor`** (~169). Presheaf-level left unitor =
  `MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor`; relies on `𝟙_ ≡ PresheafOfModules.unit` (defeq),
  `SheafOfModules.unit_val`. → fold under `lem:tensorMod_unitor`.
- **`sheafifyValIso`** (~185). `sheafification.obj A.val ≅ A` via the reflective
  adjunction counit (`PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction`, counit `IsIso` by
  reflectivity). **Reusable** beyond the unitor (will be reused by `tensorAssoc`). →
  its own small `def:` block.
- **`tensorRightUnitor`** (~195). `A ⊗ 𝒪_X ≅ A` = `tensorBraiding A (unit) ≪≫
  tensorLeftUnitor A`. `lem:tensorMod_unitor` prose already mentions the right unitor
  "follows by braiding" → fold into that block or give it its own.

## Blocked — do NOT re-assign (no structural change yet)
- **`tensorAssoc`** — blocked until `sheafifyTensorComparison` exists (verified-absent
  Mathlib analogue; same blocker would recur). Build the comparison FIRST.
- **`H_locallyFree_of_ext_vanishing`** (`IsLocallyFreeOfFiniteRankOn`),
  **`ext_finite_flat`** 2nd conjunct (`IsLFPAndFlatOn`), **`exists_acyclic_surjection`**
  (`extGroup`) — still conclude an **opaque** Prop. P1b-iii: give those stubs real
  defs first (next def target `IsLocallyFreeOfFiniteRankOn`, gates 1.3).
- **`exists_free_resolution_step`** (1.4) — real conclusion but needs
  fp-sheaf⇒free-presentation SES + pullback-of-SES; `External.descent_noetherian` is
  too weak. Defer behind free-presentation infra; do NOT tempt a new axiom.

## Reusable proof patterns discovered (also in PROJECT_STATUS KB)
- **`erw` bridges `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` ↔ `ConcreteCategory.hom`.** When a
  `tensorObj_map_tmul` (`*.Hom.hom`) goal must meet a `braiding_hom_apply`
  (`ConcreteCategory.hom`) lemma, `simp`/`simp only` will NOT close it; a single `erw
  [tensorObj_map_tmul]` does (defeq swap on pure tensors). Pattern for any
  sheafify-a-presheaf-braiding naturality.
- **Reflective-counit iso needs an UN-pinned type** — annotating the codomain of a
  `sheafification`-counit `asIso` breaks `IsIso` synthesis (re-routes
  `[HasWeakSheafify]/[WEqualsLocallyBijective]`). Leave it inferred.
- **Presheaf unit/unitors/associator are defeq-reachable** (`𝟙_ ≡
  PresheafOfModules.unit`; `MonoidalCategory.{leftUnitor,associator}` apply at presheaf
  level) — only the *sheafified* tensor needs hand-work via the comparison iso.

## Doctor / housekeeping
- 6 `External.*` axioms re-flagged again (expected boundary). Standing user action:
  mark them protected in `archon-protected.yaml` to silence the per-iter flag. Carried
  in TO_USER.
